summary: Deep research on long-running autonomous workflows / harness + graph engineering; Symphony + CC ports, augment loop engineering, marketplace inventory; synthesize for the plan

cluster_findings.summary: This cluster (OpenAI Symphony + its ports OpenSymphony, cymphony, stokowski, plus the "harness engineering" doctrine) all implement ONE durable pattern: a headless poll-loop "conductor" that watches a work source (Linear board), and for each work item spawns a SINGLE coding agent in an isolated, persistent per-issue workspace, advancing it through a typed state machine with human gates. Crucially, "multi-agent" here means multi-ISSUE concurrency (a worker pool across a board), NOT a team of agents collaborating on one task — there is no internal fan-out or shared blackboard between agents. Durability is deliberately database-free: in-memory scheduler state is thrown away on restart and re-derived from the tracker (canonical state) + persistent workspaces on disk + agent session resume (claude -p --resume <session_id>). Verification is externalized to CI/PR-review/complexity/walkthrough gates and to human state-transitions on the tracker. The harness-engineering post supplies the quality substrate the whole thing assumes: repo as single source of truth, "success is silent / only failures are verbose," file-size limits, structured logging, shared utility packages. For our Fable-5 + graphify design the conductor/durability/state-machine/human-gate mechanics port cleanly; the Linear coupling, the no-fan-out worker model, and the punted cost-routing do NOT.
key_patterns: DURABILITY (database-free, tracker-driven recovery): Symphony SPEC.md keeps 'in-memory scheduler state with filesystem-driven recovery' and explicitly does NOT restore exact scheduler state on restart — on boot it re-polls the tracker for active issues and re-dispatches. Persistence = three cheap layers: (a) tracker holds canonical work state, (b) deterministic per-issue workspace dir survives on disk (~/.cymphony/workspaces/<proj>/<issue-id>), (c) agent session resume. No Postgres, no event log.

PERSISTENT PER-TASK WORKSPACE as the context checkpoint: every issue gets a deterministic path with a sanitized+entropy-hashed key (SPEC invariants: run agent only inside the per-issue path, path must stay inside workspace root). cymphony: 'reuses the workspace per-issue, the agent keeps full context across rounds ... you don't pay for a fresh re-read of the codebase every iteration.' Restart finds the same checkout+branch and continues.

SESSION RESUME for multi-turn continuity across restarts: stokowski runs `claude -p --output-format stream-json --resume <session_id>`; continuation turns send only NEW guidance, not the original prompt (SPEC: 'Continuation turns send only new guidance'). This is the cheap resume primitive.

TYPED STATE MACHINE with two layers (Symphony SPEC): a claim/scheduling layer (Unclaimed → Claimed → Running → RetryQueued → Released) preventing duplicate dispatch, and a run-attempt lifecycle (PreparingWorkspace, BuildingPrompt, LaunchingAgentProcess, InitializingSession, StreamingTurn, Finishing, Succeeded, Failed, TimedOut, Stalled, CanceledByReconciliation). Distinct terminal reasons drive distinct retry logic.

POLL-LOOP CONDUCTOR with a fixed per-tick sequence (SPEC): 1 reconcile running issues, 2 dispatch preflight validation, 3 fetch candidate issues, 4 sort by dispatch priority, 5 dispatch while slots remain. Default cadence 30s (cymphony 5s). Sorting: priority asc → oldest created_at → identifier lexicographic.

RECONCILIATION every tick (self-healing): re-fetch tracker snapshots for all running IDs; if upstream state went terminal → kill worker + clean workspace; if still active → refresh in-memory snapshot; refresh failure keeps workers running and retries next tick. This is how an autonomous loop stays consistent with a changing world.

STALL DETECTION + EXPONENTIAL BACKOFF: kill a silent agent after stall_timeout_ms (default 5 min) and queue a retry; abnormal exit backoff = min(10000 * 2^(attempt-1), max_retry_backoff_ms) capped 300s; normal exit schedules a ~1s continuation retry to re-check tracker. Retries re-validate eligibility before re-dispatch.

CONCURRENCY as layered slot caps: global max_concurrent_agents, per-STATE caps (max_concurrent_agents_by_state), and per-PROJECT fairness caps (stokowski). available_slots = max(global - running, 0). Hierarchy-aware in OpenSymphony: 'parent issues wait for sub-issues to complete' (a work DAG).

PER-STATE MODEL/HARNESS ROUTING (the cost-routing seam): stokowski — 'Opus for investigation, Sonnet for implementation, Codex for a second opinion on code review — all in the same run,' per-state runner/model overrides in workflow.yaml. OpenSymphony routes cost via OPENSYMPHONY_HARNESS (Codex app-server on ChatGPT sub vs managed OpenHands) and LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_MODEL env.

HUMAN GATES as tracker state transitions + comments-as-feedback (no bespoke UI): stokowski adds explicit Gate states with approve/rework transitions and rework_to loops back to an earlier state; feedback is Linear comments the agent re-reads on next poll. Durable crash-recovery via structured HTML comments on the issue (stokowski/tracking.py).

THREE-LAYER PROMPT ASSEMBLY (stokowski/prompt.py): global prompt (shared codebase context) + stage prompt (per-state Jinja2 template) + auto-injected lifecycle metadata (issue id/description, transitions, rework feedback). Keeps headless agent prompts SEPARATE from interactive CLAUDE.md so autonomous directives don't bleed into dev sessions.

WORKFLOW-AS-VERSIONED-POLICY, hot-reloaded: WORKFLOW.md / workflow.yaml carries YAML front-matter config (poll interval, concurrency, active/terminal states, model) + a Markdown prompt body, in-repo and version-controlled; changes re-apply at runtime without restart (SPEC 'dynamic reload').

LIFECYCLE HOOKS around each run: after_create (clone), before_run (prep/pull; failure aborts the attempt), after_run (test/cleanup; failure logged+ignored), before_remove (teardown); receipt files (.opensymphony.after_create.json) confirm hook completion before the agent proceeds.

VERIFICATION GATES externalized: Symphony landing requires 'CI status, PR review feedback, complexity analysis, and walkthrough videos.' The agent must produce structured evidence of completion. Approval posture is implementation-defined (SPEC: auto-approve exec + file-change for the session; treat user-input-required turns as HARD FAILURE — an autonomous run must never block on a prompt).

MEMORY SUBSTRATE (OpenSymphony): .opensymphony/memory/memory.yaml preserves learned patterns; `memory capture/related --area/sync-docs` CLI; a local DuckDB index backs querying with NO external service. sync-docs promotes stable knowledge into public docs — a read-through memory that graduates.

HARNESS-ENGINEERING doctrine (OpenAI post, via search + humanlayer): ~1M LOC shipped by Codex agents with the human designing the ENVIRONMENT not the code. Rules: repository is the single source of truth ('anything the agent cannot access in-context effectively does not exist'); structured logging; naming conventions; file-size limits; prefer shared utility packages over hand-rolled helpers (centralize invariants); back-pressure + verification; 'success is silent, only failures produce verbose output'; sub-agents for context control to prevent context rot; fix real failure modes iteratively rather than pre-designing the ideal config.

CONTROL PLANE for observability (OpenSymphony): opensymphony_control exposes GET /healthz, /api/v1/snapshot, /api/v1/capabilities; token totals (input/output) surfaced in snapshots; a separate TUI process for live human monitoring — orchestration and observation are decoupled processes.
borrow_or_avoid: BORROW database-free tracker+filesystem recovery — for a long-running research loop, checkpoint the plan/DAG to the graphify KB (our 'tracker') + keep a deterministic per-research-task workspace on disk; re-derive live state on restart instead of persisting scheduler internals.

BORROW the deterministic persistent per-task workspace — one dir per research question that survives restarts so a resumed lane 'keeps full context across rounds' and never re-reads the corpus from scratch (directly matches read-through-KB economics).
BORROW claude -p --resume <session_id> + 'continuation turns send only new guidance' — the cheap multi-turn resume primitive for our executor lanes across restarts.

BORROW the two-layer typed state machine (claim states + run-attempt lifecycle) — gives our lane supervisor a precise vocabulary (Running/RetryQueued/Stalled/CanceledByReconciliation) and prevents duplicate dispatch of the same task.

BORROW the per-tick reconcile+stall-detect+backoff loop — self-healing autonomy: kill silent lanes after a timeout, exponential-backoff retry, and reconcile against the KB each tick so the outer loop stays consistent.

BORROW per-STATE model routing (Opus investigate / Sonnet implement / Codex second-opinion) — this is exactly our Fable-5 lane doctrine expressed as config; make model+lane a per-stage field in the workflow, and treat cost routing as first-class since Symphony's SPEC punts it.

BORROW human-gate-as-state-transition + comments-as-feedback — cheapest durable escalation: a 'needs-human' state in the KB whose resolution feeds back on next tick, no bespoke UI; and the SPEC rule 'user-input-required turn = hard failure' so an autonomous lane never silently blocks.

BORROW three-layer prompt assembly and keep headless prompts OUT of interactive CLAUDE.md (stokowski) — global + stage + lifecycle-injection; prevents autonomous directives leaking into human dev sessions (respects our claude_md_import_stub constraint).
BORROW workflow-as-versioned-in-repo-policy with hot reload — maps to our skills/rules; the pipeline definition (stages, gates, models, concurrency) lives in git, not in code.

BORROW the harness-engineering substrate rules — 'repo/KB is the single source of truth,' 'success is silent / only failures verbose,' file-size limits, structured logging, shared utilities — these ARE the design spec for our verify-gate oracle and the read-through KB.
AVOID hard-coupling to Linear/any SaaS tracker as the canonical state store — our blackboard is the graphify KB + files; do not import the GraphQL-tracker dependency.

AVOID Symphony's no-fan-out, no-shared-blackboard worker model — it parallelizes across ISSUES, not across agents on one task; a research framework specifically WANTS intra-task fan-out + a shared KB, so copy the conductor but add the collaboration layer Symphony lacks.

AVOID throwing away ALL scheduler state on restart for long-horizon research — Symphony can re-derive from a small board, but a multi-hour research DAG should checkpoint partial progress (completed sub-questions, gathered sources) to the KB so a restart resumes mid-plan, not from tick zero.
AVOID treating cost routing as out-of-scope (Symphony SPEC explicitly does not address it) — make it a designed component, not an env-var afterthought.

AVOID the heavyweight daemon framing (Elixir app-server / cargo binary) — the Python ports (stokowski ~orchestrator.py/runner.py/tracking.py) prove a lightweight poll-loop is sufficient and fits our python/ + mise-task house style.
applies_to_plan: READ-THROUGH KB: the persistent-per-task-workspace + session-resume pattern is the mechanical basis for 'the library is the only search' — a resumed research lane reads its accumulated KB workspace instead of re-fetching; OpenSymphony's memory.yaml + DuckDB + `sync-docs` (graduate stable knowledge to docs) is a concrete model for how graphify entries should promote from scratch to canonical.

COMPOUNDING LOOP: the poll-tick sequence (reconcile → validate → fetch → sort → dispatch) + reconciliation + stall/backoff retry is a ready-made outer-loop skeleton; add KB-checkpointing of the plan DAG so each cycle compounds on durable partial results rather than re-deriving.

LANE SUPERVISOR: adopt the two-layer state machine and per-state model routing directly — each Fable-5 lane (codex/antigravity/Opus) is a 'runner' with a per-stage model override in an in-repo workflow file; borrow os.killpg process-group management + stall-timeout kill (stokowski) for lane lifecycle; enforce the SPEC's 'user-input-required = hard failure' so lanes escalate to a human-gate state instead of hanging.

OUTER LOOP / VERIFY-GATE ORACLE: externalized verification (CI/PR-review/complexity/walkthrough + 'success is silent, only failures verbose') is exactly the architect's verify-before-done gate; keep verification a separate reconciliation pass that only surfaces failures, and require structured evidence of completion before a task leaves the loop.

HUMAN ESCALATION: implement a KB 'gate' state with approve/rework transitions and comments-as-feedback so escalation is durable and resumable without a bespoke UI — the architect verifies evidence, and an unmet gate parks the task for human input then re-enters on the next tick.

COST ROUTING (gap to fill): Symphony leaves it unspecified — our design must own it; per-stage model routing is the seam, and the architect-plans / executor-implements / Opus-fallback split maps to Symphony's Opus-investigate/Sonnet-implement/Codex-review routing.

FABLE-5 vs fable-orchestrator: Symphony is issue-parallel (a worker pool over a board) while our fable-orchestrator is within-task delegation (one architect, many executor lanes) — they are COMPLEMENTARY. Port Symphony's conductor as the OUTER long-running loop that feeds tasks to the fable-orchestrator as the INNER per-task executor, giving durability+scheduling on top of the delegation we already have.

cluster_findings.summary: Augment Code's "loop engineering" is the practice of designing systems where a team of specialist agents drives an outcome end-to-end (investigate → modify → test → review), pulling humans in only at the checkpoints where judgment has the highest leverage. The canonical unit is a five-stage loop — Trigger → Execution → Verification → Outcome → Improve — with a tight inner mini-loop between Execution and Verification that iterates until acceptance criteria are met OR the agent hits an escalation boundary (the explicit stop condition that separates "shipped" from "spinning and burning tokens"). Loops compose: one loop's outcome triggers downstream loops (ticket→PR spawns code-review, incident→postmortem spawns remediation), forming a compound system. The operational substrate is Remote Agents / the "Cosmos" orchestration platform: isolated containerized workers that wake from a queue, run async (up to ~10 in parallel), lean on a proprietary Context Engine (structural codebase map + vector index, real-time re-indexed on every push) instead of grep, and emit review-ready PRs behind a human merge gate. The measured objective is explicitly "outcomes per dollar, not output per dollar."
key_patterns: FIVE-STAGE LOOP as the atomic unit (blog): Trigger → Execution → Verification → Outcome → Improve. Each stage is nameable, instrumented, and independently tunable — this is the skeleton for any long-horizon autonomous run.

INNER VERIFY↔EXECUTE MINI-LOOP with an explicit stop condition (blog): 'the agent continues to execute and verify until it meets its acceptance criteria OR reaches a point where it needs to escalate.' The escalation boundary is a first-class design object, not an error path — it is what stops a loop from burning tokens forever.

SPECIALIST-AGENT COMPOSITION over monolith (blog + product): production loops are 'multiple specialist agents, each with its own objective, tools, and acceptance criteria.' Cosmos names concrete roles — Work Dispatcher (triage), PR Author (implementation), Code Review Fleet (three specialized reviewers), Tester (verification). Each owns a slice end-to-end and hands off to the next.

QUEUE-BASED WAKE/SLEEP WORKERS (Remote Agents guide): stateless 'lightweight service' workers 'wake up, grab a task from a queue, and go back to sleep when finished,' scaling horizontally on Kubernetes/serverless — durability lives in the queue + index, not in a long-lived agent process.

ISOLATED PER-TASK ENVIRONMENT (product): every Remote Agent gets its own workspace, repo copy, and virtualized OS — sandboxed execution prevents cross-contamination; a Shared File System at tenant/user tiers is the deliberate collaboration seam on top of isolation.
EVENT + CRON + WEBHOOK TRIGGERS (guide): SDLC triggers on GitHub/Jira/CI/Slack events; cron schedules ('0 3 * * 0'); CI job-status webhooks. Wakeup is multi-source, not just human prompt.

CONTEXT ENGINE AS KNOWLEDGE SUBSTRATE (guide + product): structural codebase map + vector index over dependency graphs, docs, and commit history (up to ~500k files/repo); 'distills only the relevant slices of context' cutting token use ~33%. Real-time indexing patches the index 'within seconds of every push' so agents 'never rewrite code that's already changed on another branch.'

VERIFICATION AS GATED PIPELINE, not vibes (guide): keep main green + branch protection + CI must pass; staging environment mirroring prod before deploy; PRs opened for human review with inline findings, test results, and screenshots. Human always owns the merge decision.

IMPROVE STAGE / TRACE-DRIVEN REFINEMENT (blog): every run emits a trace of 'what worked, what failed, and where the human stepped in and corrected course'; teams feed those traces back to refine prompts, tests, and escalation rules. The loop tunes itself over runs.
COMPOUND / CHAINED LOOPS (blog): a loop's outcome is another loop's trigger (ticket→PR→code-review; incident→postmortem→remediation). Long-horizon autonomy = a DAG of small loops, not one giant loop.
COST-AS-OUTCOME METRIC (blog): measure 'outcomes per dollar, not output per dollar' — the routing/budget target is verified results, which makes the escalation boundary economically load-bearing.

EMERGENCY-STOP + AUDIT PLANE (guide): feature flags gate high-risk operations as an emergency stop; structured JSON logs queryable by repo/workflow/model-version flow to SIEM so 'every decision the agent makes' is traceable line by line.
borrow_or_avoid: BORROW the five-stage loop (Trigger→Execution→Verification→Outcome→Improve) as the literal shape of our research loop — it maps cleanly onto our compounding loop + outer loop.

BORROW the inner verify↔execute mini-loop with an explicit escalation boundary — this IS our verify-gate oracle's stop condition; make 'escalate vs keep-iterating' a first-class decision, not a failure.
BORROW trace-driven Improve: persist every run's what-worked/what-failed/where-human-corrected trace and feed it back to refine prompts/gates — our graphify KB is the natural home for these traces.

BORROW the Context Engine's 'distill only relevant slices' + real-time re-index-on-change principle — it is exactly our read-through KB where the library is the only search; keep the graph fresh on every ingest so lanes never act on stale context.
BORROW queue-wake/sleep stateless workers: put durability in the queue + KB, not in a long-lived agent, so a killed lane loses nothing (mirrors our incremental-persistence rule).
BORROW per-task isolation + a deliberate shared-state seam (their Shared File System) — our lanes should run isolated but read/write one blackboard (the KB), not ad-hoc scratch.
BORROW compound/chained loops: model long-running research as a DAG of small loops (one outcome triggers the next) rather than a single unbounded agent.
BORROW 'outcomes per dollar' as the cost-routing objective — route lanes (codex/antigravity/Opus) toward verified results, not raw token throughput.

AVOID treating the Augment blog as an implementation spec — it is conceptual; it names NO durable-workflow engine (no Temporal/Cadence), no checkpointing, no mid-task resume semantics. We must design our own durability layer.

AVOID pure-stateless design for OUR case: their workers assume the queue + index hold all state; a long-horizon research run needs explicit checkpointing/resume that they do not describe — do not inherit the gap.
AVOID an unbounded fan-out assumption — they cap parallelism (~10 agents) and use resource reservations to prevent conflicts; we should bound lane concurrency similarly rather than spawn freely.
AVOID leaving escalation implicit — the guide admits no concrete escalation policy (no Slack/incident hooks); we must specify exactly when/how a lane hands back to the Claude architect/human.
applies_to_plan: READ-THROUGH KB: Augment's Context Engine validates the core bet — a structural map + fresh index that 'distills only relevant slices' beats keyword search and cuts tokens ~33%. Our graphify KB should be the sole retrieval surface for lanes, re-indexed on every ingest 'within seconds' so no lane acts on stale knowledge.

COMPOUNDING LOOP: adopt their inner verify↔execute mini-loop verbatim — each research iteration executes, the verify-gate checks acceptance criteria, and it either loops or escalates; the Improve stage writes the run trace back into the KB so the next iteration starts smarter.

LANE SUPERVISOR: their specialist-agent composition (Work Dispatcher / PR Author / Review Fleet / Tester) is a template for our supervisor routing Claude-architect → codex/antigravity/Opus lanes, each with its own objective + acceptance criteria + tools, each owning a slice and handing off.

OUTER LOOP: model the long-running autonomy as compound/chained loops (one outcome triggers the next) driven by multi-source wakeups (event + cron + webhook) over queue-wake/sleep workers — durability in the queue+KB — with 'outcomes per dollar' as the routing objective and an explicit escalation boundary back to the Claude architect (the human/oracle checkpoint Augment leaves under-specified).

FABLE-5-FIRST SEQUENCING: the orchestrator we ship first already needs the specialist-lane composition + verify-gate + escalation boundary; these are reused unchanged when the outer autonomous loop is layered on — exactly the reuse the task intends.

cluster_findings.summary: This cluster teaches that long-horizon autonomous orchestration is a HARNESS problem, not a model problem: reliability comes from scaffolding around the model (durable state, execution graphs, verification gates, context compaction, memory substrates), and "every component exists because the model can't do it alone." Sol-Orchestrator is the single most directly reusable design: a delegating-supervisor where a DURABLE GOAL is decoupled from disposable WORKFLOW GRAPHS ("one goal, many workflows"), workers run in isolated context and their output lands in a REVIEW state (never silently "done"), and the supervisor waits on events rather than polling. awesome-harness-engineering is a taxonomy of the primitives we'll need — plan/execute separation, hibernate-and-wake checkpointing, AST/symbol-indexed context (120x token cuts), MCP code-execution (98.7% token cuts), tool-annotation risk vocab, hook-pipeline governance, and eval/CI gates. The community marketplace confirms this is a crowded, converging design space: dozens of plugins already implement pieces we plan (typed knowledge-graph memory with decay, memory buses, disk-backed handoff, fresh-subagent-per-plan, wave parallelism, loop/failure triage) — strong signal to borrow proven shapes and avoid reinventing state plumbing. The dominant lesson for our Fable-5-first plan: make the ORCHESTRATOR's context sacred (materialize only chosen evidence), make state DURABLE and graph-shaped, and make "done" mean "passed a verification gate," with the graphify KB as the read-through memory substrate.
key_patterns: Sol: durable-goal / disposable-workflow split — a persistent goal object survives across many versioned workflow graphs ('one goal, many workflows'); replanning = workflow_replace mints a new graph version without losing the goal or git state

Sol: execution graph as a real DAG — steps/jobs carry explicit dependencies, ownership, and parallelism; causally-unrelated jobs stay parallel and the supervisor synthesizes their evidence before cascading

Sol: review-gate lifecycle — a finished worker job moves to REVIEW, not 'done'; supervisor accepts (workflow_complete), requests correction, or replaces (workflow_retry). 'Done' is gated, never silent

Sol: protected orchestrator context — worker transcripts stay OUT of the supervisor's conversation; agents_inspect materializes only the specific diff/result/tool-output the supervisor asks for (the read-through-evidence discipline)

Sol: event-driven wait, not polling — agents_wait blocks on a meaningful event; workflow_status exposes semantic next-actions; goal_block pauses at genuine human/external boundaries (escalation) and /goal-stop is the escape hatch that stops workers without reverting git
Sol: profile-tier cost routing — Sol(decisions) / Luna-Medium(narrow) / Terra-Medium(cross-file) / Terra-Max(hard/ambiguous), each with configurable model+reasoning; task complexity picks the actor
Sol: compaction companion — OpenCode Operational-Checkpoint persists decisions + current work + bounded workflow state so a compressed/hibernated session resumes without losing orchestration context
harness: hibernate-and-wake checkpointing lets a 6-hour task resume without context loss; AgentSPEX expresses this as declarative YAML workflows with typed steps, branching, and explicit checkpoints
harness: plan/execute separation — Plan.md/Implement.md artifacts, planner-once + executor-loop, LATS/MCTS search over trajectories with backtracking on failure

harness: context compaction stack — server-side compaction (84% cut), LLMLingua 20x, BM25 retrieve-on-demand (context-mode), AST/tree-sitter symbol indexing (120x, codebase-memory-mcp), and MCP code-execution where the agent writes code to call tools (98.7% token cut)
harness: filesystem-as-interface — Azure SRE finding that 'expose everything as files + read_file/grep' beat specialized tooling; Mirage mounts S3/Slack/GitHub as a virtual FS the agent greps

harness: tool-annotation risk vocabulary (readOnly / destructive / idempotent / openWorld) feeds harness/permission decisions; hook pipelines (SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse) do governance deterministically
harness: verification as first-class — eval gates in CI, trajectory logging/replay, three-judge verification, self-review checklists; unit-test-style evals fail, simulation/trajectory evals work

harness: memory/state handoff — initializer→executor handoff uses feature lists + git commits + test gates AS the durable state; persistent Progress.md / Knowledge blocks consolidated on agent trigger

plugin agent-knowledge: typed knowledge graph (11 edge types, directed BFS traversal) + hybrid semantic+TF-IDF search + confidence/decay scoring + auto session distillation — a concrete read-through-KB memory shape
plugin agent-memory: cross-agent memory BUS + auto-compression of tool output into structured observations + token-budgeted injection (only relevant memory per prompt)
plugin agentsatlas: runs each 3-5-task plan in a FRESH subagent so quality doesn't degrade over a long horizon — context-reset-per-unit as a durability tactic
plugin agentops / agent-estimate: wave-based parallel execution with METR reliability thresholds and PERT estimation to plan fan-out waves
plugin agent-trace-triage: deterministic loop detection + classify failures into fixed buckets + auto triage report — the observability/self-healing layer for long runs
plugin agenticmail: email/SSE as the agent wake/coordination bus with per-agent WAKE BUDGETS — an economic throttle on autonomous fan-out
borrow_or_avoid: BORROW Sol's durable-goal / disposable-workflow split — our outer loop should own a persistent goal in the KB while lane workflows are cheap, versioned, replaceable graphs
BORROW Sol's review-gate lifecycle — the Claude architect is exactly the 'move to review, then accept/correct/replace' oracle; wire 'done' to a verify-gate, never to a worker's self-report

BORROW Sol's protected-context + agents_inspect — the architect must NOT ingest raw codex/antigravity transcripts; pull only the chosen diff/evidence, matching our 'library is the only search' read-through discipline
BORROW Sol's event-wait + goal_block — model the lane supervisor on agents_wait (no polling) and a hard human-escalation boundary, not a timeout loop
BORROW hibernate-and-wake + AgentSPEX-style typed checkpoints — the outer loop needs resumable checkpoints (decisions+work+graph state) so a multi-hour run survives compaction/restart
BORROW agent-knowledge's typed-graph-with-decay shape for the graphify second brain — confidence/decay scoring + BFS traversal is a proven read-through-KB memory model
BORROW agentsatlas's fresh-subagent-per-plan — reset executor context every N tasks to stop long-horizon quality decay; cheaper than fighting context rot

BORROW MCP code-execution + AST symbol indexing for context economy — let executors write code to call tools and query the KB by symbol rather than dumping files (the 98.7%/120x wins map straight onto our token/cost routing)

BORROW tool-annotation risk vocab + hook-pipeline governance — our verify-gate and permission model should key off readOnly/destructive/idempotent, enforced by deterministic hooks (mirrors this repo's PreToolUse guard)
BORROW wave-based fan-out with wake budgets (agentops/agenticmail) — cap concurrent autonomous lanes with an explicit economic budget so the outer loop can't runaway-spend
AVOID building a bespoke agent-comm/message-bus from scratch — agent-synapse/agent-comm/agenticmail show it's solved; if we need inter-lane comms, adopt a shape, don't hand-roll state plumbing
AVOID transcript-passing between lanes — the recurring failure mode this cluster warns against is polluting the orchestrator's context; keep evidence materialization explicit and narrow
AVOID polling/sleep-based scheduling for wakeups — every mature design here is event-driven (agents_wait, SSE, hook triggers); polling burns tokens and hides state

AVOID equating 'skipped/finished' with 'verified' — Sol's review state and the harness eval-gate literature both insist a completed job is a claim to be checked, matching our probes-need-a-control-arm rule

AVOID over-adopting marketplace plugins as dependencies — treat them as design references; most are single-author, overlapping, and would inject schema/context cost. Borrow patterns, vendor selectively
applies_to_plan: Read-through KB: graphify becomes the substrate agent-knowledge/agent-recall prototype — store the durable GOAL, decisions, and evidence as a typed graph with confidence/decay; the architect queries the KB (BFS/path) instead of grepping, making 'the library the only search'. Persist worker evidence into the KB per Sol's agents_inspect (only the chosen result), not full transcripts

cluster_findings.summary: The 2026 state of the art converges on a clear layering for long-horizon autonomous agents: a durable-execution engine underneath (Temporal/Restate/DBOS/Inngest, or LangGraph's checkpointer at the agent layer) that journals every nondeterministic boundary and replays to resume after crash; a "harness" wrapped around the model (constraints, tools, verification gates, feedback loops, lifecycle/entropy management) that is now recognized as a distinct engineering discipline where reliability comes from the system, not the model; a blackboard / shared-state substrate for multi-agent coordination that lets specialized agents volunteer against shared state rather than being centrally dispatched; and a knowledge graph as the compounding memory substrate, built by a Read-Search-Verify-Construct (RAGA) loop that moves correctness to write-time with provenance and bi-temporal stamps. Cost-aware model routing (40-85% bill reduction reported) and a four-tier human-escalation model (read-only / reversible / external / irreversible) with async-durable approvals round out the picture. The orchestration-topology debate ("graphs vs loops") resolves to hybrids: a planned graph/DAG at the top with bounded agent loops in the lanes and mandatory verification checkpoints — almost exactly the Fable-5 architect-plus-executor-lanes shape. The strongest cross-cutting lesson: durability, verification, and provenance must be write-time and incremental, never end-of-run.
key_patterns: DURABILITY — Journal + replay boundaries (Zylos): give each run a stable run_id and each op a step_id; treat every LLM call, tool call, shell command, file write, approval, and outbound message as a REPLAY BOUNDARY whose result is recorded and reused on recovery. Recovery reads the journal, replays completed steps from stored results, resumes from the last boundary — mutations are NOT re-executed.

DURABILITY — Idempotent tool wrappers (Stripe-style keys) + the Outbox pattern: wrap every mutating tool with an idempotency key + receipt + duplicate detection; write durable intent and local state in ONE transaction, then a separate dispatcher completes the external side effect exactly once. Prevents double-writes across crash/API boundaries.

DURABILITY — Determinism split (Temporal): keep orchestration code deterministic, push all nondeterminism/side-effects into 'Activities' (Restate: ctx.run steps; Inngest: step.run memoization). This is the invariant that makes replay-based resume possible at all.

SCHEDULING/WAKEUP — Awakeables / durable external-event waits (Restate) + durable sleeps/timers: an agent can pause for hours/days waiting on a human approval or webhook WITHOUT occupying a live process, then resume with replayed context. This is the mechanism for long-horizon 'sleep then wake' loops.

STATE/BLACKBOARD — Shared blackboard coordination (arXiv 2510.01285): a control agent posts requests to shared state; autonomous subordinate agents VOLUNTEER based on current blackboard content, so the coordinator need not know each agent's expertise. Event-driven activation, 13-57% end-to-end gain over baselines. PatchBoard (2605.29313) adds schema-grounded state mutation for auditable/reliable writes.

MEMORY SUBSTRATE — Knowledge graph as agent memory (two 2026 surveys, arXiv 2602.05665 / 2602.06052): graphs natively give structured + temporal + traceable memory (KGs, temporal graphs, hypergraphs, hierarchical trees) across an extract/store/retrieve/evolve lifecycle; the graph evolves continuously rather than storing static histories — the substrate for continuous learning + self-reflection.

COMPOUNDING LOOP — RAGA / Read-Search-Verify-Construct (Vadim autonomous-KG): one agent owns the graph lifecycle. Read candidate edges with evidence spans; Search resolves endpoints to existing concepts (skip present edges); Verify checks grounding + closed-vocabulary schema + verbatim evidence span + a confidence gate (~0.6); Construct emits CREATE-only. Every write is a function of current graph state — correctness moves to write time, eliminating the batch dedup pass.

PROVENANCE — Bi-temporal, never-hard-delete: every edge carries {confidence, reason, source, evidence} plus valid_at/recorded_at/invalid_at. Superseded facts are soft-invalidated (stamp invalid_at), never deleted — the graph keeps rollback/audit provenance. Advisory-by-default: construction is autonomous but publication is human-gated.

HARNESS ENGINEERING (as a discipline) — the harness = everything except the model: constraints/architectural boundaries, tools, verification gates, feedback loops, observability, lifecycle scaffolding, and 'entropy management' (scheduled refactor/GC agents that prevent long-running drift). Reliability comes from the wrapper, not model swaps (LangChain traced failures to lift Codex 52.8%->66.5% with NO model change).

VERIFICATION — Hybrid deterministic + agent-as-verifier gates: combine linters/type-checkers/schema-validators (cheap, deterministic) with agent-reviewing-agent checks; convert every one-off fix into a permanent reusable constraint (new lint rule / sub-agent). A static DAG-level verifier (INFRAMIND / 2605.05657) can reject infeasible orchestration configs BEFORE any LLM call.

COST ROUTING — Query-conditioned + confidence-aware model routing: route by complexity/difficulty/risk signals; agents self-report confidence and the orchestrator reassigns subtasks live (Orchestrating Intelligence 2601.04861). Reported 40-85% bill reduction with no visible quality drop. Caveat: static-feature routers (MasRouter) can't tell an idle model from a saturated one — add load/infra awareness (INFRAMIND).

HUMAN ESCALATION — Four-tier risk model (read-only / reversible / external / irreversible): reserve mandatory approval only where mistake-cost exceeds automation value. Async-durable approval is the production standard (sync collides with timeouts/token-expiry); persist the EXACT artifact under review (version hash) so replay can't approve modified content. Review the decision, not the whole run; progressive trust lowers approval frequency as an agent proves reliable.

ORCHESTRATION TOPOLOGY — Graphs vs loops resolves to hybrid: a planned graph/DAG when work branches across specialized agents and state must persist; a bounded loop with a clear stop condition for single-scoped tasks. Best practice (Linear 'Loops', Andrew Ng 'Three Loops'): scheduled entry points + bounded agent tasks + mandatory human-review checkpoints — match orchestration to workflow shape, not ideology.

INCREMENTAL-CAPTURE INVARIANT (cross-cutting): durable capture must be write-time and incremental — the journal, the graph writes, the recovery-test crash-injection suite, and agent report persistence all fail if deferred to end-of-run. An agent that dies at 13/20 sources should leave 13, not 0.
borrow_or_avoid: BORROW journal+replay-boundary durability — model the outer loop as a run_id/step_id journal so a killed lane or crashed architect resumes from the last boundary instead of re-running codex/Opus calls (which cost real money and re-trigger side effects).
BORROW idempotent tool wrappers + outbox — every graphify write, every PR/commit/ship action, every codex dispatch gets an idempotency key so a resume never double-writes the KB or double-opens a PR.

BORROW awakeables / durable external-event waits — implement the human-escalation gate and 'wait for CI' as a durable pause that survives process death, not an in-turn blocking wait (matches the repo's own 'don't wait blind' rule and the Mac-side reaping hazard).

BORROW the RAGA Read-Search-Verify-Construct loop verbatim — it IS our compounding-KB loop: Search-before-write against the existing graphify graph is exactly the 'library is the only search / read-through KB' idea; Verify-before-Construct with an evidence-span gate is our verify-oracle at write time.

BORROW bi-temporal never-hard-delete provenance — soft-invalidate superseded KB facts (valid_at/invalid_at) so the second-brain keeps audit/rollback; aligns with the repo's agent-report-persistence + provenance-of-numbers rules (a fact needs its source AND its condition).

BORROW hybrid deterministic+agent verification gates — the Claude architect's 'verify evidence before done' should layer a deterministic gate (mise run lint / verify / schema check) UNDER the agent-review, and every recurring failure becomes a permanent constraint (hk step / suites.toml contract) — the repo already does this.

BORROW confidence-aware + risk-tiered cost routing — route lanes by task difficulty/risk (Opus/codex/antigravity), let lanes self-report confidence, and reserve the expensive terminal-fallback lane for low-confidence/high-risk subtasks.
BORROW harness 'entropy management' — schedule a periodic refactor/GC agent for the KB and the codebase; long-running autonomy accumulates cruft and stale edges without it.

BORROW the blackboard volunteer pattern for the lane supervisor — post tasks to shared state and let lanes claim by capability, rather than the architect hard-coding which lane does what; improves flexibility as lanes are added.

AVOID open-ended unbounded agent loops with no stop condition or checkpoint — the graphs-vs-loops debate is explicit that branching work becomes 'an unmanageable pile of if-statements'; keep lanes bounded and checkpointed.

AVOID trusting model self-reported confidence as calibrated probability — the RAGA source flags this explicitly; use it as a routing/gate signal only, always backed by a deterministic verifier or control-arm probe.

AVOID synchronous / end-of-run approval and end-of-run capture — sync approval dies on timeouts; end-of-run capture loses everything on crash. Both contradict durability; use async-durable gates and incremental writes.
AVOID static-feature-only routing — a router blind to lane load/saturation (MasRouter critique) will over-schedule a busy lane; add infra/load awareness.
AVOID naive post-hoc dedup of the KB — RAGA shows search-before-write at write-time removes the batch dedup pass entirely and prevents the graph from proliferating duplicate concepts.
applies_to_plan: READ-THROUGH KB — RAGA's Search stage is the concrete design for 'the library is the only search': before any external fetch or write, query the existing graphify graph; only Read/Construct what is genuinely new. This makes the KB the primary retrieval surface and keeps writes idempotent, matching the repo's graphify-query-first rule.

COMPOUNDING LOOP — adopt Read-Search-Verify-Construct as the KB-ingest loop with an evidence-span + closed-vocabulary + confidence gate at Verify, CREATE-only at Construct, and bi-temporal soft-invalidation. This gives a KB that provably compounds (correctness at write-time) rather than accreting noise across long runs.

LANE SUPERVISOR — model lanes on the blackboard volunteer pattern + confidence/risk cost routing: post tasks to shared state, route to codex/antigravity/Opus by difficulty and load, let lanes self-report confidence, and have the Claude architect run the hybrid deterministic+agent verification gate before 'done'. The static DAG-level verifier (reject infeasible plans before any LLM call) is a cheap pre-check for the architect's plan.

OUTER LOOP (long-running autonomy, built AFTER Fable-5 ships) — put a durable-execution engine under the whole thing: journal every LLM/tool/ship/KB-write as a replay boundary; use awakeables for human-escalation and CI-wait pauses that survive process death; enforce a global retry budget across the run. DBOS is the lightest fit (in-process Postgres library, zero new infra, OpenAI-Agents integration) if the team wants durability without standing up Temporal; LangGraph's checkpointer is the agent-layer alternative. This directly solves the repo's known Mac-side 'backgrounded task gets reaped' hazard — a durable pause is not a live process.

HUMAN ESCALATION — wire the four-tier risk model into the architect's verify-gate: read-only/reversible lanes run autonomously; external/irreversible actions (ship, land, merge, KB publish) require async-durable approval that persists the exact artifact + version hash. Progressive trust reduces gates as lanes prove reliable — an explicit knob for increasing autonomy over time.

HARNESS/ENTROPY — schedule a periodic GC/refactor agent over both the KB (invalidate stale edges) and the orchestrator's own rules/config, since the whole system is designed for long-horizon unattended runs where drift is the dominant failure mode.

INCREMENTAL DURABILITY — the repo's existing agent-report-persistence 'write incrementally, never at end-of-run' rule is the same invariant the durable-execution literature enforces at the engine level; the outer loop should make it structural (journal-backed) not just a documented discipline.

result.synthesis:
# Consolidated Brief: Long-Running Autonomous Multi-Agent Orchestration
*(for the graphify-second-brain + Fable-5-orchestrator plan — design input, not build)*

> Four substantive clusters synthesized (Symphony/CC-ports/harness-doctrine; Augment loop-engineering; Sol-Orchestrator/awesome-harness/marketplace; 2026 durable-execution SOTA). The "diagnostic" stub cluster is a test artifact and is discarded.

---

## 1. Highest-value patterns to design toward

**P1 — Journal + replay-boundary durability (the resume substrate).**
*Sources:* Zylos durable-execution (run_id/step_id journaling); Temporal/Restate/Inngest/DBOS/LangGraph checkpointer; Symphony's cheaper DB-free variant.
*Maps to:* **native-primitives outer loop.** Treat every LLM call, codex/Opus dispatch, tool call, KB write, and ship/land action as a replay boundary with a recorded result; a killed lane or crashed architect resumes from the last boundary instead of re-paying for calls or re-triggering side effects. Lightest fit = DBOS (in-process Postgres, zero new infra) or LangGraph checkpointer; Symphony proves a DB-free filesystem+tracker recovery is *sufficient* for smaller DAGs. Directly kills the repo's known Mac-side "backgrounded task gets reaped" hazard — a durable pause is not a live process.

**P2 — Persistent per-task workspace + session-resume (context checkpoint).**
*Sources:* Symphony/cymphony/stokowski (`~/.cymphony/workspaces/<proj>/<issue>`, `claude -p --resume <session_id>`, "continuation turns send only new guidance"); Augment isolated per-task env + Shared File System seam.
*Maps to:* **read-through KB + lane supervisor.** One deterministic dir per research question that survives restarts so a resumed lane keeps full context and never re-reads the corpus. Session-resume is the cheap multi-turn primitive for executor lanes across restarts.

**P3 — Two-layer typed state machine + review-gate lifecycle ("done" is gated, never self-reported).**
*Sources:* Symphony SPEC (claim states + run-attempt lifecycle: Running/RetryQueued/Stalled/CanceledByReconciliation); Sol-Orchestrator (worker output lands in REVIEW → accept/correct/replace); Augment inner verify↔execute mini-loop with explicit escalation boundary.
*Maps to:* **lane supervisor + verify-gate oracle.** Each Fable-5 lane is a runner; jobs return to a REVIEW state the Claude architect gates. Gives precise vocabulary that prevents duplicate dispatch and makes "escalate vs keep-iterating" a first-class decision, not an error path. SPEC rule: **user-input-required turn = HARD FAILURE** — an autonomous lane escalates to a gate state, never silently blocks.

**P4 — Event-driven scheduling: reconcile / stall-detect / backoff, wait-don't-poll.**
*Sources:* Symphony poll-tick (reconcile→validate→fetch→sort→dispatch, stall kill after 5min, exp backoff capped 300s); Sol `agents_wait` + `goal_block`; Restate awakeables / durable external-event waits; Augment queue-wake/sleep + cron/webhook/event triggers.
*Maps to:* **outer loop.** Self-healing autonomy: reconcile lane state against the KB each tick, kill silent lanes, backoff-retry. Prefer durable awakeables (survive process death) for the human-gate and CI-wait pauses over in-turn blocking. Every mature design here is event-driven; polling burns tokens and hides state.

**P5 — Knowledge graph as compounding memory via Read-Search-Verify-Construct (RAGA), write-time correctness.**
*Sources:* Vadim autonomous-KG (RAGA loop, evidence-span + closed-vocab + ~0.6 confidence gate, CREATE-only, bi-temporal never-hard-delete); 2026 KG-memory surveys (2602.05665/06052); agent-knowledge plugin (typed graph, 11 edge types, BFS, confidence/decay); OpenSymphony memory.yaml+DuckDB+`sync-docs`; Augment Context Engine (structural map + fresh index, ~33% token cut, re-index "within seconds of every push").
*Maps to:* **read-through KB + compounding loop.** RAGA's **Search stage IS "the library is the only search"** — query graphify before any external fetch/write; only Read/Construct what's genuinely new. Verify-before-Construct is the verify-oracle at *write time* → eliminates the batch dedup pass. Bi-temporal soft-invalidation keeps audit/rollback (aligns with the repo's provenance-of-numbers rule: a fact needs source AND condition). `sync-docs` = model for how KB entries promote from scratch → canonical.

**P6 — Cost routing as first-class: per-stage model + confidence/risk-aware, load-aware.**
*Sources:* stokowski (Opus-investigate / Sonnet-implement / Codex-review per-state); Sol profile tiers (Sol/Luna/Terra); "Orchestrating Intelligence" 2601.04861 (confidence-aware live reassignment, 40–85% bill cut); INFRAMIND (reject infeasible plans pre-LLM; static-feature routers can't see load); Augment "outcomes per dollar, not output per dollar."
*Maps to:* **lane supervisor.** This is exactly the Fable-5 architect-plans / executor-implements / Opus-fallback split expressed as per-stage config. **Symphony's SPEC explicitly punts cost routing — so we must OWN it as a designed component, not an env-var afterthought.** Route by difficulty/risk, let lanes self-report confidence (as a routing signal only, never as calibrated probability), reserve expensive Opus fallback for low-confidence/high-risk subtasks.

**P7 — Verification as a separate, deterministic-under-agent, control-armed gate.**
*Sources:* Symphony externalized gates (CI/PR-review/complexity/walkthrough); harness-engineering "success is silent, only failures verbose"; hybrid deterministic+agent-as-verifier (LangChain 52.8→66.5% with NO model change); INFRAMIND static DAG verifier; three-judge/self-review; agent-trace-triage (loop detection + 5 failure buckets).
*Maps to:* **verify-gate oracle.** Layer a cheap deterministic gate (`mise run lint`/`verify`/schema check) UNDER agent-review; every recurring failure becomes a permanent constraint (hk step / suites.toml contract) — the repo already does this. Matches probes-need-a-control-arm: a completed job is a *claim to be checked*, and "skipped/finished ≠ verified."

**P8 — Four-tier human escalation with async-durable approval + progressive trust.**
*Sources:* digitalapplied four-tier model (read-only / reversible / external / irreversible); Sol `goal_block`; Symphony human-gate-as-state-transition + comments-as-feedback; agentiqa/anticipating-shadow-points pre-mortem gates.
*Maps to:* **outer loop + escalation.** Read-only/reversible lanes run autonomously; external/irreversible actions (ship, land, merge, KB publish) require **async-durable** approval that persists the exact artifact + version hash (so replay can't approve modified content). Progressive trust is the explicit knob for *increasing autonomy over time*. Cheapest durable form: a "needs-human" KB state whose resolution feeds back next tick — no bespoke UI.

**Cross-cutting invariant (all four clusters agree):** durability, verification, and provenance must be **write-time and incremental, never end-of-run.** An agent that dies at 13/20 sources must leave 13. This is the repo's existing agent-report-persistence rule made structural (journal-backed).

## 2. BORROW vs AVOID

**BORROW**
- **Symphony/CC-ports:** DB-free tracker+filesystem recovery (KB = our tracker); deterministic persistent per-task workspace; `claude -p --resume` + new-guidance-only continuations; two-layer state machine; per-tick reconcile+stall+backoff; per-state model routing; human-gate-as-state-transition; three-layer prompt assembly (keep headless prompts OUT of interactive CLAUDE.md — respects `claude_md_import_stub`); workflow-as-versioned-in-repo-policy with hot reload; `os.killpg` process-group lifecycle; "user-input-required = hard failure."
- **Augment loop-engineering:** the five-stage loop (Trigger→Execution→Verification→Outcome→Improve) as literal shape; inner verify↔execute mini-loop with explicit escalation boundary; trace-driven Improve stage (persist what-worked/failed/where-human-corrected into the KB); Context Engine "distill only relevant slices + real-time re-index"; queue-wake/sleep stateless workers; compound/chained loops (DAG of small loops); "outcomes per dollar."
- **Sol / harness / SOTA:** durable-goal / disposable-workflow split ("one goal, many workflows"); review-gate accept/correct/replace; **protected orchestrator context + `agents_inspect`** (materialize only chosen evidence — never ingest raw lane transcripts); `agents_wait` event-wait; hibernate-and-wake typed checkpoints (AgentSPEX); journal+replay + idempotent tool wrappers + outbox; awakeables for durable pauses; RAGA loop verbatim; bi-temporal never-hard-delete; blackboard *volunteer* pattern for the lane supervisor; harness "entropy management" (scheduled GC/refactor agent over KB + rules); wave-fan-out with explicit wake/spend budgets.
- **Marketplace (as design references, not deps):** agent-knowledge typed-graph-with-decay shape; agentsatlas fresh-subagent-per-plan (context reset per N tasks to stop long-horizon decay); agent-memory compression + token-budgeted injection; agent-trace-triage self-healing observability; agentops/agenticmail wake budgets; agent-handoff disk-backed plan→execute→verify.

**AVOID**
- **Symphony's no-fan-out worker model** — it parallelizes across *issues*, not agents on one task; research WANTS intra-task fan-out + shared KB. Copy the conductor, add the collaboration layer.
- **Hard-coupling to Linear / any SaaS tracker** as canonical state — our blackboard is graphify KB + files.
- **Throwing away ALL scheduler state on restart** — fine for a small board, wrong for a multi-hour research DAG; checkpoint partial progress so restart resumes mid-plan, not tick-zero.
- **Treating cost routing as out-of-scope** (Symphony's explicit gap) — own it.
- **Transcript-passing between lanes / polluting the architect's context** — the recurring failure mode; keep evidence materialization explicit and narrow.
- **Polling/sleep scheduling, synchronous approval, end-of-run capture** — burn tokens, die on timeouts, lose everything on crash. Use event-wait, async-durable gates, incremental writes.
- **Unbounded agent loops with no stop condition/checkpoint** — "unmanageable pile of if-statements"; bound + checkpoint every lane.
- **Static-feature-only routing** (MasRouter critique) — add load/saturation awareness.
- **Trusting model self-reported confidence as calibrated** — routing signal only, always backed by a deterministic verifier/control-arm.
- **Naive post-hoc KB dedup** — RAGA search-before-write removes it entirely.
- **Over-adopting marketplace plugins as dependencies** — single-author, overlapping, inject schema/context cost; borrow shapes, vendor selectively.
- **Treating the Augment blog as an implementation spec** — conceptual only; names no durable-execution engine, no checkpointing/resume. We design our own durability layer (don't inherit their stateless-worker gap).
- **Heavyweight daemon framing** (Elixir app-server / cargo binary) — the Python ports prove a lightweight poll-loop suffices and fits the repo's `python/` + mise-task house style.

## 4. How this changes the plan (Phase 2+, Fable-5-first)

**Sequencing stays Fable-5-first.** Symphony/Augment/Sol all confirm the split: **Fable-5 orchestrator = the INNER per-task executor** (architect + codex/antigravity/Opus lanes + verify-gate + escalation boundary); the **long-running autonomous OUTER loop is layered on top** and reuses those components unchanged. Everything Phase-1 already needs (specialist-lane composition, verify-gate, escalation boundary, protected architect context) is reused verbatim when Phase 2 wraps it in durability + scheduling.

**Concrete additions to the Phase 2+ (outer-loop) spec:**

1. **Add a durability layer as an explicit component** (was under-specified). Journal every LLM/codex/Opus/tool/KB-write/ship as a run_id/step_id replay boundary; idempotent tool wrappers (key + receipt) on every graphify write and every PR/commit/ship so resume never double-writes. Recommend **DBOS** (in-process Postgres, zero new infra, OpenAI-Agents integration) or LangGraph checkpointer; keep Symphony's DB-free filesystem+KB recovery as the lightweight fallback. **This is the structural fix for the repo's Mac-side "backgrounded task gets reaped" hazard** — durable pauses aren't live processes.

2. **Make cost routing a designed component, not an env-var** (Symphony's explicit gap). Per-stage model+lane field in the in-repo workflow file; confidence/risk/load-aware routing; static DAG-level feasibility check (INFRAMIND) as a cheap pre-LLM plan gate.

3. **Adopt RAGA (Read-Search-Verify-Construct) as the KB-ingest loop** — Search-before-write against graphify = "the library is the only search"; evidence-span + closed-vocab + ~0.6 confidence gate at Verify; CREATE-only at Construct; bi-temporal soft-invalidation. Removes the batch-dedup pass entirely and makes the KB *provably* compound.

4. **Model the lane supervisor on Sol** — durable-goal / disposable-workflow split; jobs return to REVIEW (architect gates accept/correct/replace); `agents_wait` event-wait (no polling); **`agents_inspect`-style narrow evidence materialization** — the architect never ingests raw lane transcripts. Add the **blackboard volunteer pattern** so lanes claim tasks by capability rather than being hard-dispatched (scales as lanes are added).

5. **Wire the four-tier escalation model into the verify-gate** — read-only/reversible autonomous; external/irreversible (ship/land/merge/KB-publish) require async-durable approval persisting the exact artifact + version hash. Add a **progressive-trust knob** (lower approval frequency as lanes prove reliable) as the explicit autonomy-increase mechanism.

6. **Add trace-driven Improve + entropy management** — persist every run's what-worked/what-failed/where-human-corrected trace into the KB (Augment); schedule a periodic GC/refactor agent over both the KB (invalidate stale edges) and the orchestrator's own rules/config (harness discipline) — drift is the dominant long-horizon failure mode.

7. **Enforce fan-out bounds + wake/spend budgets** — cap concurrent lanes (Augment ~10; Symphony layered slot caps: global/per-state/per-project); agentsatlas fresh-subagent-per-plan to stop long-horizon quality decay; explicit economic wake budget so the outer loop can't runaway-spend.

8. **Make incremental durability structural** — the repo's existing agent-report-persistence "write incrementally, never end-of-run" rule becomes journal-backed at the engine level, not just a documented discipline.

**Net:** Phase 1 ships Fable-5 (inner executor). Phase 2 adds, in priority order: (a) durable-execution journal + idempotent wrappers, (b) RAGA read-through KB loop, (c) designed cost-router, (d) Sol-style event-driven lane supervisor with blackboard volunteering, (e) four-tier async-durable escalation + progressive trust, (f) trace-driven Improve + entropy GC.
